body:0001.text: This article is machine translated from english

body:0003.text: The AI Analysis feature helps admins quickly understand, categorize, and analyze large volumes of resident input. It works for both ideation and surveys, offering:

body:0004.text: Qualitative analysis: Summarizing textual input from ideas, comments, or survey responses

body:0005.text: Quantitative analysis : Exploring correlations between demographics, tags, and survey responses

body:0006.text: ℹ️ Access to this feature depends on your plan.

body:0008.text: The interface has four main columns:

body:0009.text: Tag Management: Create and manage tags to group inputs

body:0010.text: Input List: Shows all contributions to analyze

body:0011.text: User & Filter View: View anonymized user responses, apply filters, or drill into comments

body:0012.text: Summaries & Questions: Summarize filtered inputs or ask AI for insights

body:0013.text: How to use AI analysis for ideation/proposals?

body:0015.text: Click the blue Open AI Analysis widget

body:0016.text: Summarize inputs and comments (scroll to the comment section in column 3)

body:0017.text: Use filters (e.g., by demographics) to narrow focus before summarizing

body:0018.text: How to use AI analysis for surveys?

body:0020.text: Scroll down to the question you want to analyze

body:0021.text: Click Explore to add the questions to the analysis

body:0022.text: Optionally, add follow-up questions for deeper analysis

body:0023.text: How to filter and preview inputs?

body:0024.text: Filter by timeframe, engagement level, or demographics

body:0025.text: Preview how answers distribute across demographics (only for users who completed their profile)

body:0026.text: ℹ️ Engagement level filter is only available for ideation/proposals projects

body:0027.text: How to use summarize and ask a question?

body:0028.text: Use Summarize to create a digest of selected inputs

body:0029.text: Use Ask a Question to probe further

body:0030.text: Check in-line references and validate with original inputs

body:0031.text: Drag summaries into platform or project reports (summaries appear in the “AI” tab)

body:0033.text: Tagging helps cluster and interpret user input. The tool supports:

body:0034.text: Fully automated tagging: AI assigns common tags.

body:0036.text: By example: Provide manual examples to teach AI tagging.

body:0037.text: Sentiment tagging: Mark input as positive or negative.

body:0039.text: Manual tagging: For full control, you can tag every input manually.

body:0041.text: Auto‑Insights connects tags, demographics, and survey questions to uncover patterns.

body:0042.text: Displays key correlations (e.g., which age group engages with which tag)

body:0043.text: Includes a heatmap view, with boxes marked if a correlation is statistically significant

body:0044.text: Unit of analysis can be switched between:

body:0050.text: In ideation, Auto‑Insights connects demographics to tags

body:0051.text: In surveys, Auto‑Insights links demographics, tags, and survey questions

body:0052.text: You can get into the auto-insights by pressing explore below the demographic information of the AI tool interface.

body:0054.text: The Summarize and Ask a Question tools generate quick insights but require human oversight.

body:0055.text: Summaries include clickable references to original inputs for transparency

body:0056.text: Overloading the AI with too many inputs can reduce accuracy—filters & tags help

body:0057.text: Missing in-line references may happen for reasons like:

body:0061.text: ⚠️ Summaries are not 100% accurate. Always cross-check key findings with the original data.

body:0063.text: For a complete demonstration of our AI Analysis tool, check out the recording of our AI Masterclass. Select your language for the appropriate recording.
